title: Launch assets, announcement posts, and post-launch flywheel
short_description: Produce the launch assets with the tool itself (2-3 minute flagship crude cut, 60-second flat explainer, contact sheets and verify JSON published alongside), draft Show HN and r/Python posts for Arnav's approval and post them, and stand up the verify-passed-but-human-flagged issue template with first-week triage into constraint-rule issues.
long_description_md:
# Launch assets, announcement posts, and post-launch flywheel

## Why this task exists

Last three done-when bullets of `docs/cofounder/ROADMAP.md` Phase 8: launch assets produced by the tool itself (flagship crude episode 2-3 min cut, 60s flat explainer, contact sheets + verify JSON published alongside), Show HN + r/Python posts drafted/approved/posted, and the post-launch flywheel ("verify passed but human flagged it" issue template live, first week's reports triaged into constraint-rule issues). `go-to-market.md`'s launch plan spells out the exact assets: "a 2-3 minute flagship episode (crude pack, history topic, subtitles on), a 60-second flat-pack tech explainer, the contact sheets and the verify JSON for both." Growth loop #1 in `go-to-market.md` ("Output carries the signature") and #2 ("Verification-rule flywheel: real-world misses -> new rules -> verify gets harder to fool") both depend on this task landing correctly — the flywheel doesn't start compounding until the issue template and triage habit exist.

## Roadmap fit

Depends on `v1-flagship-episode` (the flagship crude episode's underlying project/script this task cuts down to 2-3 minutes and republishes with fresh assets), `pypi-name-and-publish` (posts must point at a real, installable package — posting before publish would be dishonest), and `docs-site-context7` (posts and the README link to the live docs site). This is the last task in Phase 8 and in the roadmap — nothing else depends on it.

## Scope

1. Render the 2-3 minute flagship crude-pack cut (history topic, subtitles on) using `anima render` against a project derived from `v1-flagship-episode`'s script/scenes (a trimmed/re-cut project, not the full 15-20 min episode), generate its contact sheet with `anima preview`, and its verify report with `anima verify --json`, publishing all three (mp4, contact sheet images, verify JSON) alongside the launch materials.
2. Render the 60-second flat-pack tech explainer as a separate short project (new short script, flat style pack), with its own contact sheet and verify JSON, published alongside.
3. Draft the Show HN and r/Python announcement posts anchored on the real flagship episode + its verify report (per `go-to-market.md`'s launch plan and acquisition channel #4), get Arnav's explicit approval since these post under his name/account, then post them in the documented order (Show HN, then r/Python + r/opensource) — external, identity-bearing action, flagged first per `COFOUNDER_PERSONA.md`.
4. Stand up the "verify passed but a human flagged it" GitHub issue template (per `go-to-market.md`'s post-launch discipline: every such report becomes a public constraint-rule issue, because visibly converging verification is the marketing) and triage the first week's incoming reports into constraint-rule issues.

## Explicitly out of scope

- PyPI name confirmation and publish — `pypi-name-and-publish`.
- Docs site build/deploy and Context7 listing — `docs-site-context7`.
- Actually implementing any new constraint rules surfaced by first-week triage — that work becomes its own future task(s) once triaged, not built inline here.
- The full 15-20 minute flagship episode itself (the v1 bar render) — `v1-flagship-episode`; this task only produces the shortened launch cut plus the separate short flat-pack explainer.

deliverables.description: Render the 2-3 minute flagship crude-pack cut (history topic, subtitles on, derived from v1-flagship-episode's project) via anima render; generate its contact sheet via anima preview and its verify report via anima verify --json; assert the render succeeds, verify status is passing, and duration falls within the 2-3 minute launch-asset bound.

verification.command: anima render launch-assets/flagship-cut --json > /tmp/flagship-render.json && anima verify launch-assets/flagship-cut --json > /tmp/flagship-verify.json && anima preview launch-assets/flagship-cut --json > /tmp/flagship-preview.json && python3 -c "import json; r=json.load(open('/tmp/flagship-verify.json')); assert r['status']=='pass', r; d=json.load(open('/tmp/flagship-render.json'))['duration_seconds']; assert 100 <= d <= 200, d; print('flagship cut OK', d)"

deliverables.description: Render the separate 60-second flat-pack tech explainer via anima render; generate its contact sheet via anima preview and verify report via anima verify --json; assert the render succeeds, verify status is passing, and duration is approximately 60 seconds.

verification.command: anima render launch-assets/flat-explainer --json > /tmp/flat-render.json && anima verify launch-assets/flat-explainer --json > /tmp/flat-verify.json && anima preview launch-assets/flat-explainer --json > /tmp/flat-preview.json && python3 -c "import json; r=json.load(open('/tmp/flat-verify.json')); assert r['status']=='pass', r; d=json.load(open('/tmp/flat-render.json'))['duration_seconds']; assert 50 <= d <= 75, d; print('flat explainer OK', d)"

deliverables.description: GitHub issue template for 'verify passed but a human flagged it' reports exists in .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE with the required structured fields (project/seed reference, attached verify JSON, style pack, description of what verify missed), so the post-launch flywheel has a real, structured intake surface from day one.

Draft Show HN and r/Python posts anchored on the real flagship episode and its verify report, get Arnav's explicit approval since posting is under his name, post them in order (Show HN then r/Python + r/opensource), and triage the first week's incoming 'verify passed but flagged' reports into public constraint-rule issues — external, identity-bearing action, flagged to Arnav first per COFOUNDER_PERSONA.md.

verification.steps: 1. Draft the Show HN post and r/Python (+ r/opensource) post as markdown, each anchored on the flagship episode link, its contact sheet, and its verify JSON, referencing the live PyPI package and docs site. 2. Present both drafts to Arnav for explicit approval before posting anything — this is external publication under his name/account per COFOUNDER_PERSONA.md's narrow exception. 3. Once approved, post Show HN first, then r/Python and r/opensource, in that order per go-to-market.md. 4. Log the posts (URLs, timestamps) in docs/cofounder/DECISIONS.md. 5. Over the following week, monitor the 'verify passed but human flagged it' issue template intake and file each genuine report as a public constraint-rule issue (or confirm zero reports arrived, logged either way).
